# UC-22.59.13 · Maritime cyber-incident detection + flag-State + RO + USCG NRC + port-State 24-hour reporting clock — automated multi-authority notification with submission receipts

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Security, Compliance &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*If a ship has a cyber incident, the company has 24 hours to tell the flag country, the class society, and the coast guard. We start a clock the moment an incident is confirmed, watch each reporting deadline, and raise the alarm if anything is running late so the deadline is never missed.*

---

## Description

Tracks every confirmed maritime cyber-incident through the multi-authority reporting clock; computes elapsed time per authority; raises a notable at 20-hour at-risk and 24-hour breach thresholds; classifies authority requirements per vessel based on flag State, US-port visit, and PSC MoU region.

## Value

A missed reporting deadline is itself an SMS major-non-conformity at the next DoC verification. Multi-authority reporting is operationally complex; without an automated clock, fleet operators routinely miss the 24-hour window on at least one authority during real incidents. This UC closes the operational gap.

## Implementation

(1) Wire the incident-classification workflow (ServiceNow SIR, Splunk SOAR case) to write a row into `imo_incident_register_lookup` at the moment of classification. (2) Submit notifications to flag State / RO / USCG NRC / port-State via Splunk SOAR playbooks; capture submission receipts via HEC. (3) Schedule UC-22.59.13 every 10 minutes; route at severity P1 for any breach-* or at-risk-* status.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.59.13` archived every 10 minutes to `audit_evidence` with `imo_clause="IMO-MSC-FAL-Circ-3-s2-4"`. Every row pins (incident_id, imo_number, classified_at, flag_state_notified_at, ro_notified_at, uscg_nrc_notified_at, psc_notified_at, hours_to_authority, sla_status).

## Control test

### Positive scenario

A confirmed cyber-incident on a US-port-visiting vessel is classified at T+0. UC-22.59.13 MUST track the clock and flag sla_status=at-risk when more than 20 hours have elapsed without USCG NRC notification.

### Negative scenario

Cyber-incident classified at T+0 and all four authorities notified within 6 hours. UC-22.59.13 MUST record sla_status=on-time.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- UC-22.59.1, 22.59.2, 22.59.5, 22.59.6 in production.
- Splunk SOAR with playbooks per authority and HEC tokens for submission-receipt capture.
- ServiceNow SIR or equivalent case-management system with `imo_in_scope` flag.

## Step 1 — Incident-register KV Store

```ini
[imo_incident_register_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.incident_id = string
field.imo_number = string
field.ship_name = string
field.flag_state = string
field.recognised_organisation = string
field.us_port_visit = string
field.in_psc_mou_region = string
field.imo_in_scope = string
field.classified_at = time
field.flag_state_notified_at = time
field.ro_notified_at = time
field.uscg_nrc_notified_at = time
field.psc_notified_at = time
```

## Step 2 — SOAR playbook integration

A single classification event in SIR / SOAR triggers four parallel playbook branches: (1) Flag State portal submission; (2) Class society RO portal submission; (3) USCG NRC submission (when applicable); (4) Port-State authority (Paris / Tokyo MoU) submission. Each branch captures the receipt via HEC.

## Step 3 — Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.59.13: IMO Cyber-Incident Reporting Clock]
cron_schedule = */10 * * * *
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.59.13,reg=IMO-MSC-428-98,clause=IMO-MSC-FAL-Circ-3-s2-4
alert.severity = 5
```

## Step 4 — Runbooks

**`sla_status=breach-*`** (owner: DPA + CISO, severity P1): Within 30 minutes, the DPA initiates manual notification via emergency channel (phone, fax); document for the next DoC verification.

**`sla_status=at-risk-*`** (owner: DPA, severity P2): Within 1 hour, the DPA confirms the SOAR playbook progress; manual fallback if needed.

## Step 5 — Maritime-specific notes

- **Multi-flag fleet**: vessels under different flags need different submission portals; the SOAR playbook routes per flag-State.
- **PSC MoU regions**: Paris, Tokyo, Vina del Mar, Caribbean, Mediterranean, Indian Ocean, Riyadh, Black Sea, Abuja MoUs each have their own portal.
- **NIS2 overlap**: an EU-flagged vessel may also be in scope for the company's NIS2 obligations; cross-reference UC-22.2 to avoid double reporting.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup imo_incident_register_lookup
| where imo_in_scope = "true"
| eval now_ts = now()
| eval hours_since_classification = round((now_ts - classified_at) / 3600, 2)
| eval flag_state_hours = case(isnotnull(flag_state_notified_at), round((flag_state_notified_at - classified_at) / 3600, 2), 1=1, null)
| eval ro_hours = case(isnotnull(ro_notified_at), round((ro_notified_at - classified_at) / 3600, 2), 1=1, null)
| eval uscg_hours = case(isnotnull(uscg_nrc_notified_at), round((uscg_nrc_notified_at - classified_at) / 3600, 2), 1=1, null)
| eval psc_hours = case(isnotnull(psc_notified_at), round((psc_notified_at - classified_at) / 3600, 2), 1=1, null)
| eval flag_required = "true"
| eval ro_required = "true"
| eval uscg_required = if(us_port_visit = "true", "true", "false")
| eval psc_required = if(in_psc_mou_region = "true", "true", "false")
| eval sla_status = case(
    flag_required = "true" AND isnull(flag_state_notified_at) AND hours_since_classification > 24, "breach-flag",
    ro_required = "true" AND isnull(ro_notified_at) AND hours_since_classification > 24, "breach-ro",
    uscg_required = "true" AND isnull(uscg_nrc_notified_at) AND hours_since_classification > 24, "breach-uscg",
    psc_required = "true" AND isnull(psc_notified_at) AND hours_since_classification > 24, "breach-psc",
    isnull(flag_state_notified_at) AND hours_since_classification > 20, "at-risk-flag",
    isnull(uscg_nrc_notified_at) AND uscg_required = "true" AND hours_since_classification > 20, "at-risk-uscg",
    isnotnull(flag_state_notified_at) AND (uscg_required = "false" OR isnotnull(uscg_nrc_notified_at)), "on-time",
    1=1, "in-window")
| where sla_status NOT IN ("on-time")
| sort 0 -hours_since_classification
| table incident_id imo_number ship_name classified_at hours_since_classification flag_required flag_state_hours ro_required ro_hours uscg_required uscg_hours psc_required psc_hours sla_status
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) Open incident clock (hours since classification). (Panel 2) Per-authority notification cadence. (Panel 3) SLA-breach trend (last 12 months). (Panel 4) Top flag-States by reporting volume.

## Known False Positives

1. **Multi-authority parallel reporting**: a single incident reaching all four authorities may briefly show as `at-risk-uscg` while the SOAR playbook completes; allow a 5-minute settling window. 2. **Reportable threshold ambiguity**: minor incidents may not require flag-State notification; ensure the classification step explicitly sets imo_in_scope=true. 3. **Time-zone offsets**: ship clock vs UTC ambiguity; canonicalise to UTC at classification.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `imo_22_59_13_exceptions` with the DPA + CISO documented approval.

## References

- [IMO MSC-FAL.1/Circ.3 Rev.2 §2.4 Respond](https://wwwcdn.imo.org/localresources/en/OurWork/Security/Documents/MSC-FAL.1-Circ.3-Rev.2.pdf)
- [USCG MSIB 002-23 — Reporting maritime cyber incidents](https://www.uscg.mil/Maritime-Commons/)
- [ParisMoU PSC Cyber Risk Management — 2023 Concentrated Inspection Campaign](https://www.parismou.org/)
